Name: Diego
Age & grade: 11, 6th grade.

Interests & passions:
Barcelona FC is the centre of his world. He memorises lineups, can recite the
score of every El Clasico since he started watching, and has strong opinions
about who should be in midfield against Real Madrid. He follows La Liga,
Champions League, and the Copa del Rey closely. Knows the squad numbers and
can tell you which player wears which boot brand. Outside soccer he's into
Dragon Ball Z (Goku and Vegeta, especially), and reads superhero manga on
the weekends. Plays street football with his cousins most afternoons.

Hobbies & media:
- Watching every Barcelona match live, even the away games at odd hours.
- Playing FIFA on PS5 — always plays as Barcelona, sets up exhibition
  matches against Real Madrid and Bayern Munich.
- Watching Dragon Ball Z reruns and the more recent Dragon Ball Super.
- Reading One-Punch Man and My Hero Academia manga.
- Following soccer YouTube channels and reaction videos after big games.

Learning style & teacher notes:
Quick with numbers when they're attached to something concrete he cares
about — he can compute possession percentages, goal differences, and pass
completion rates from a match scoreboard in his head. The same numbers in
an abstract worksheet ("if x + 3 = 7…") lose him fast. He's confident in
class, sometimes too confident — he answers fast without checking, then
sulks if corrected publicly. Best move: anchor every operation in a match
or a player stat first ("if Barcelona scored 3 goals across 6 shots, what
fraction of shots went in?"), let him solve in soccer terms, then walk
backwards to the abstract notation. Pair work with a quieter student
helps him slow down. Avoid Real Madrid jokes — he takes them personally.
